race.comment: READY TO SHINE (10) raced wide but kept finding and got up for a gallant third over 1100m at Canterbury last start. Fitter for two runs back from a spell and definitely needs this trip now. Has won third-up before so he should be peaking for this. WHO BUT ROO (2) went about as well as can be expected against Joliestar and co in the Expressway last time. Beaten narrowly in a $50k open sprint at the Sapphire Coast the run before which is decent form for a race like this. Can play a major role. ZALE (8) stalked the pace and tried hard but found one better over 1100m at Hawkesbury recently. The winner of four from 11 at 1200m. Drops sharply in the weights and is a chance if allowed to dictate. WALLENDA (1) has forgotten how to win - returning to midweek company is a wise move and the blinkers on for the first time may add some spark. XPRESSO (3) turned in an eye-catcher first-up at Canterbury. Hasn't won in ages but over a bit more ground today the veteran should be about the money.
